workflow: Set review_decision to accepted or rejected for each candidate.
For accepted entries, edit curation fields only if the title/summary/type needs correction.
Keep standard_id, section_id, evidence_citation, source_page_nos, and source_chunk_ids traceability intact.
Rejected entries should include review_notes explaining category error, weak evidence, duplicate, or low operational value.

curation.title: T&R Logic Application for VAV Static Pressure Control
curation.summary: Example sequence: Td=5 min, T=2 min, I=2, SPtrim=-10 Pa, SPres=15 Pa, SPres-max=37 Pa. Setpoint trims down when requests ≤ 2, responds up when requests > 2, capped at 37 Pa per step.
curation.equipment_scope: VAV air handler with multiple downstream zones
curation.trigger_condition: Fan status ON, after 5 min delay.
curation.required_behavior: Every 2 min: if R≤2, decrease setpoint by 10 Pa; if R>2, increase setpoint by (R-2)*15 Pa but max 37 Pa.
curation.evidence_quote: If R≤I, c hange Setpoint by SPtrim If R > I, change setpoint by (R – I)*SPres but no large r than SPres -max The following is an example of a sequence that uses T&R to control the static pressure setpoint of a VAV AHU serving multiple downstream zones. This sequence defines the T&R variables as shown in Informative Table 5.1.14.4. Informative Tab le 5.1.14.4 Example Sequence T&R Variables Variable Definition Device Supply fan SP0 120 Pa (0.5 in. of water) SPmin 37 Pa (0.15 in. of water) SPmax 370 Pa (1.50 in. of water) Td 5 T 2 I 2 SPtrim –10 Pa (–0.04 in. of water) SPres 15 Pa (0.06 in. of water) SPres -max 37 Pa (0.15 in. of water) Description of General Operation Starting 5 minutes after the fan status indicates the supply fan is ON, the sequence will slowly reduce the AHUs static pressure setpoint by 10 Pa (0.04 in. of water) every 2 minutes if R≤I .
curation.judge_reason: Directly captures a control sequence example with configurable parameters from Section 5.1.14.4 of Guideline 36.

curation.title: Trim & Respond Set-Point Reset Logic
curation.summary: T&R logic resets a setpoint at a fixed rate until a downstream zone is no longer satisfied and generates a request. When sufficient requests are present, the setpoint is increased. When requests subside, the setpoint resumes decreasing.
curation.equipment_scope: Air handler or plant controlling pressure, temperature, or other variables
curation.trigger_condition: Associated device is proven ON, starting Td after initial device start command.
curation.required_behavior: Every time step T, if R ≤ I, trim setpoint by SPtrim. If R > I, respond by changing setpoint by (R – I)*SPres but no larger than SPres-max.
configurable_values: SPres (respond amount, opposite sign to SPtrim)
curation.evidence_quote: 5.1.14.4. Trim & Respond logic shall reset the setpoint within the range SPmin to SPmax. When the associated device is OFF, the setpoint shall be SP0. The reset logic shall be active while the associated device is proven ON, starting Td after initial device start command. When active, every time step T, if R≤I, trim the setpoint by SPtrim. If there are more than I requests , respond by chan ging the setpoint by SPres *(R – I), (i.e., the number of requests minus the number of ignored requests) but no more than SPres -max.
curation.judge_reason: The item describes a real control sequence (Trim & Respond logic) from section 5.1.14, which is operational knowledge per Guideline 36.

curation.title: Request-Hours Accumulator Reset
curation.summary: Request-Hours Accumulator and System Run-Hours Total are reset to zero automatically when System Run-Hours Total exceeds 400 hours, or manually by a global operator command.
curation.section_title: T&R set-point reset logic and zone/system reset requests
curation.trigger_condition: System Run-Hours Total exceeds 400 hours, or manual command.
curation.required_behavior: Reset Request-Hours Accumulator and System Run-Hours Total to zero.
curation.evidence_quote: The Request-Hours Accumulator and System Run-Hours Total are reset to zero as follows: i. Reset automatically for an individual zone/system when the System Run-Hours Total exceeds 400 hours. ii. Reset manually by a global operator command.
curation.judge_reason: Captures a specific, operational commissioning procedure regarding automatic and manual reset of request-hour accumulators based on system run-hours, which is a configurable control parameter from the guideline.

curation.title: Rogue Zone Detection via Cumulative% Request-Hours
curation.summary: A Level 4 alarm is generated if zone Importance-Multiplier > 0, Cumulative% Request-Hours > 70%, and total zone/system run hours > 40.
curation.section_title: T&R set-point reset logic and zone/system reset requests
curation.equipment_scope: Downstream zones or systems generating requests
curation.trigger_condition: Importance-Multiplier > 0, Cumulative% Request-Hours > 70%, and total run hours > 40.
curation.required_behavior: Generate a Level 4 alarm.
curation.evidence_quote: A Level 4 alarm is generated if the zone Importance-Multiplier is greater than zero, the zone/system Cumulative% Request Hours exceeds 70%, and the total number of zone/system run hours exceeds 40.
curation.judge_reason: The item captures a real fault diagnostic rule from ASHRAE Guideline 36, specifying clear operational conditions for generating a Level 4 alarm, which is grounded in the provided evidence.

curation.title: T&R Variables and Defaults
curation.summary: Define variables: Device, SP0, SPmin, SPmax, Td, T, I, SPtrim, SPres, SPres-max. Initial values defined in system/plant sequences. Values for trim, respond, time step, etc. shall be tuned to provide stable control.
curation.equipment_scope: Upstream system or plant setpoint controlled by T&R loop
configurable_values: SPres (respond amount, must be opposite in sign to SPtrim)
SPres-max (maximum response per time interval, same sign as SPres)
curation.evidence_quote: For each upstream system or plant setpoint being controlled by a T&R loop, define the following variables. Initial values are defined in system/plant sequences below. Values for trim, respond, time step, etc. shall be tuned to provide stable control. See Table 5.1.14.3.
curation.judge_reason: Captures configurable control parameters for T&R loops with tuning guidance, grounded in operational control sequences from Guideline 36.

curation.title: Chilled Water Plant Enable Logic
curation.summary: Enable plant when disabled for ≥15 min, number of Chiller Plant Requests > I (default 0), OAT > CH-LOT, and schedule active.
curation.trigger_condition: Plant disabled for ≥15 min AND Number of Chiller Plant Requests > I (default 0) AND OAT > CH-LOT AND schedule active.
curation.required_behavior: Enable plant in lowest stage.
curation.evidence_quote: 5.20.2.2. Enable the plant in the lowest stage when the plant has been disabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Chiller Plant Requests > I (I = Ignores shall default to 0, adjustable), and b. OAT>CH -LOT, and c. The chiller plant enable schedule is active.
curation.judge_reason: Captures real plant enable control sequence with specific conditions from Guideline 36 section 5.20.2.2.

curation.title: Chilled Water Plant Disable Logic
curation.summary: Disable plant when enabled for ≥15 min and either requests ≤ I for 3 min, OAT < CH-LOT – 1°F, or schedule inactive.
curation.trigger_condition: Plant enabled for ≥15 min AND (Number of Chiller Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 min OR OAT < CH-LOT – 1°F OR schedule inactive).
curation.required_behavior: Disable plant.
curation.evidence_quote: Disable the plant when it has been enabled for at least 15 minutes and: a. Number of Chiller Plant Requests ≤ I for 3 minutes, or b. OAT<CH-LOT – 1°F, or c. The chiller plant enable schedule is inactive.
curation.judge_reason: Captures a real control sequence for plant disable logic from section 5.20.2.3.

curation.title: Waterside Economizer Enable Logic
curation.summary: Enable WSE if disabled for ≥20 min and CHWRT upstream of HX > PHXLWT + 2°F. PHXLWT calculated from wetbulb, design approaches, and part load ratio.
curation.trigger_condition: WSE disabled for ≥20 min AND CHWRT upstream of HX > PHXLWT + 2°F.
curation.required_behavior: Enable waterside economizer.
curation.evidence_quote: Enable waterside economizer (WSE) if it has been disabled for at least 20 minutes and CHWRT upstream of HX is greater than the predicted heat exchanger leaving water temperature (PHXLWT) plus 2°F.
curation.judge_reason: Captures a specific waterside economizer enable control sequence with configurable parameters (timing, temperature differential) from ASHRAE Guideline 36.

curation.title: Waterside Economizer Disable Logic
curation.summary: Disable WSE when it has run for ≥20 min and CHW temp downstream of HX > CHWRT upstream of HX less 1°F for 2 min.
curation.trigger_condition: WSE enabled for ≥20 min AND CHW temp downstream of HX > CHWRT upstream of HX – 1°F for 2 min.
curation.required_behavior: Disable waterside economizer.
curation.evidence_quote: Disable WSE when it has run for at least 20 minutes and CHW temp downstream of HX is greater than CHWRT upstream of HX less 1ºF for 2 minutes

curation.title: Chiller Staging – Stage Down Condition
curation.summary: Stage down if next lower stage OPLR < SPLR_DN for 15 min and not increasing >2.5%/min, and failsafe stage up condition not true.
curation.trigger_condition: Next available lower stage OPLR < SPLR_DN for 15 minutes AND next lower stage OPLR is not increasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes AND failsafe stage up condition is not true.
curation.required_behavior: Initiate stage down.
configurable_values: SPLR_DN (calculated per chiller type and lift)
curation.evidence_quote: Stage down if both of the following are true: 1. Next available lower stage OPLR < SPLR DN for 15 minutes and next lower stage OPLR is not increasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes; and 2. The failsafe stage up condition is not true.
curation.judge_reason: This is a real control sequence from the chiller staging section of Guideline 36, capturing a specific stage down condition with configurable parameters and timing.

curation.title: Chiller Staging – Stage Up Efficiency Condition
curation.summary: Stage up if current stage OPLR > SPLR_UP for 15 min and OPLR not decreasing >2.5%/min averaged over 5 min.
curation.trigger_condition: Current stage OPLR > SPLR_UP for 15 minutes AND current stage OPLR is not decreasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes.
curation.required_behavior: Initiate stage up.
configurable_values: SPLR_UP (calculated per chiller type and lift)
curation.evidence_quote: Efficiency Condition : Current stage OPLR > SPLR UP for 15 minutes and current stage OPLR is not decreasing at a rate greater than 2.5% per minute averaged over 5 minutes

curation.title: Chilled Water Supply Temperature Reset (DP Controlled Loops)
curation.summary: Reset DP setpoint from CHW-DPmin to CHW-DPmax from 0-50% loop output, then reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin from 50-100% loop output. Trim & Respond with parameters.
curation.trigger_condition: Plant enabled.
curation.required_behavior: Reset CHW Plant Reset variable using Trim & Respond logic with given parameters.
curation.evidence_quote: From 0% loop output to 50% loop output, reset DP setpoint from CHW -DPmin to CHWP - DPmax. b. From 50% loop output to 100% loop output, reset CHWST setpoint from CHWSTmax to CHWSTmin. c. CHW Plant Reset variable shall be reset using Trim & Respond logic with the following parameters: Variable Value Device Any CHW Pump Distribution Loop SP0 100% SPmin 0% SPmax 100% Td 15 minutes T 5 minutes I 2 R Cooling CHWST Reset Requests SPtrim -2% SPres +3% SPres-max +7%
curation.judge_reason: Captures a real operational control sequence for chilled water supply temperature reset with DP control from Section 5.20.5.2.

curation.title: Primary CHW Pump Speed Control (Variable Primary-Variable Secondary with Decoupler Flow Meter)
curation.summary: Reset primary pump speed by reverse acting PID maintaining decoupler flow at 5% of PCHWFdesign. Map loop output from CH-MinPriPumpSpdStage to 100% speed.
curation.trigger_condition: Plant enabled and primary pumps proven on.
curation.required_behavior: Maintain decoupler flow at 5% of PCHWFdesign via PID loop.
curation.evidence_quote: Primary pump speed shall be reset by a reverse acting PID loop maintaining flow through the decoupler flow meter at 5% of PCHWFdesign
curation.judge_reason: Extracted control sequence for primary pump speed reset using reverse acting PID to maintain decoupler flow, directly from section 5.20.6.17.

curation.title: AFDD – Chilled Water Supply Temperature Too High
curation.summary: Fault condition FC#6: CHWST_AVG - ƐCHWT ≥ CHWSTSP. Applies to OS #2-#5.
curation.trigger_condition: CHWST_AVG - ƐCHWT ≥ CHWSTSP
curation.required_behavior: Report alarm with possible diagnosis: mechanical problem with chillers or primary flow too high.
curation.fault_condition: Chilled water supply temperature too high
curation.evidence_quote: FC#6 Equation CHWST AVG - ƐCHWT ≥ CHWSTSP Applies to OS #2 – #5 Description Chilled water supply temperature is too high
curation.judge_reason: Valid fault diagnostic rule with equation and applicable operating states

curation.required_behavior: Report alarm with possible diagnosis: condenser fouling, low CW temperature or flow.

curation.title: Primary Only Condensing Boiler Staging Down
curation.summary: Stage down if Qrequired < 110% of B-STAGE MIN of current stage for 5 min or bypass valve >0% for 5 min, failsafe not true, and Qrequired < 80% of design capacity of next lower stage for 5 min.
curation.trigger_condition: All of: 1) (Qrequired < 110% of B-STAGE MIN of current stage for 5 minutes OR minimum flow bypass valve >0% open for 5 minutes); 2) failsafe stage up condition not true; 3) Qrequired < 80% of design capacity of boilers in next lower stage for 5 minutes.
curation.required_behavior: Stage down to next available lower stage.
curation.evidence_quote: Stage down if all of the following are true: 1. Either: i. Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes; or ii. The minimum flow bypass valve, if provided, is greater than 0% open for 5 minutes. 2. The fail safe stage up condition is not true. 3. Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity, QbX, of the boilers in the next available lower stage for 5 minutes.
curation.judge_reason: Extraction captures a real control sequence with specific parameters and timers from G36 section 5.21.3.9.g.

curation.title: Variable Primary/Variable Secondary Condensing Boiler Staging Down
curation.summary: Stage down if Qrequired < 110% of B-STAGE MIN of current stage for 5 min or (primary pumps at min speed and primary HWRT > secondary HWRT by 3°F for 5 min), failsafe not true, and Qrequired < 80% of design capacity of next lower stage for 5 min.
curation.equipment_scope: Variable primary/variable secondary condensing boiler plants
curation.trigger_condition: All of: 1) (Qrequired < 110% of B-STAGE MIN of current stage for 5 minutes OR Primary HW pumps at B-MinPriPumpSpdStage and primary HWRT > secondary HWRT by 3°F for 5 minutes); 2) failsafe stage up condition not true; 3) Qrequired < 80% of design capacity of boilers in next lower stage for 5 minutes.
curation.required_behavior: Stage down to next available lower stage.
curation.evidence_quote: Stage down if all of the following are true: 1. Either: i. Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes; or ii. For 5 minutes, Primary HW pumps are at B-MinPriPumpSpdStage and primary HWRT exceeds secondary HWRT by 3°F. 2. The failsafe stage up condition is not true. 3. Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity, QbX, of the boilers in the next available lower stage for 5 minutes.
curation.judge_reason: Captures a real staging down control sequence with specific parameters and timing from the guideline.

curation.title: Hybrid Boiler Staging Down (Condensing Current Stage)
curation.summary: If all boilers in current stage are condensing, stage down using same conditions as variable primary/variable secondary condensing: Qrequired < 110% of B-STAGE MIN for 5 min or (primary pumps at min speed and primary HWRT > secondary HWRT by 3°F for 5 min), failsafe not true, and Qrequired < 80% of design capacity of next lower stage for 5 min.
curation.trigger_condition: Same as 5.21.3.9.i.
curation.required_behavior: Stage down to next available lower stage.
curation.evidence_quote: If all boilers enabled in the current stage are condensing, stage down if all of the following are true: 1. Either: i. Qrequired falls below 110% of B-STAGE MIN of the current stage for 5 minutes; or ii. For 5 minutes, Primary HW pumps are at B-MinPriPumpSpdStage and primary HWRT exceeds secondary HWRT by 3°F. 2. The failsafe stage up condition is not true. 3. Qrequired is less than 80% of the design capacity, QbX, of the boilers in the next available lower stage for 5 minutes.
curation.judge_reason: Extraction captures a real control sequence with configurable parameters and timing conditions from Section 5.21.3.9.m, providing actionable operational knowledge.
